title: Fusae Yoshida Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454994 .

Oral history interview with Fusae Yoshida, who was a pre-teen at the time of WWII. She was living in Tacoma, Washington with her family when the war broke out. Her family was sent to the concentration camp in Jerome, Arkansas then to Heart Mountain, Wyoming. Fusaw got a job as a translator helping Japanese Americans resettle before the camps closed. Her family relocated to Berkeley after the war. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
